stories.title: Metsola to rule on complaints about hate speech following deportations vote
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — European Parliament President Roberta Metsola has to decide whether to sanction two MEPs accused of hate speech after a vote last week on EU migration rules. Valérie Hayer, the head of the liberal Renew Europe group, complained in a letter to Metsola on Friday that Danish MEP Kristoffer Storm and Finnish MEP Sebastian Tynkkynen, both from the European Conservatives and Reformists group, had committed acts of “a racist nature” against Swedish MEP Abir Al-Sahlani, a bre

stories.title: Weather tracker: Extreme heat in Europe as typhoon strengthens off Philippines
stories.summary: Eastern Europe to see temperatures above early July average into next week The extreme heat experienced across central and western Europe, including the UK, will continue to shift eastwards. As slightly cooler weather infiltrates into western Europe, with risks of downpours and thunderstorms, eastern Europe is likely to see temperatures several degrees above the early July average into next week. Peak highs of between 35C and 40C are expected across southern Poland, Slovakia, Hungary and into th

stories.title: Czechia: Thousands march in support of public media funding
stories.summary: Prime Minister Andrej Babis' government has been accused of seeking to take control over Czech public media. The plan would leave broadcasters with a 15% funding cut and no guarantees of continued future funding.

stories.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
stories.summary: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.

stories.title: Az iráni válogatott az öltözőben hagyott írással üzent az amerikaiaknak és a világnak
stories.summary: A háború miatt csak különböző korlátozások mellett pályára lépő válogatott megköszönte Los Angelesnek a vendéglátást, és kiállt a béke mellett.

stories.summary: Ezzel Dél-Korea legértékesebb tőzsdén jegyzett vállalatává vált.

stories.summary: Andrej Babiš kormánya szigorúbban ellenőrizné a közmédiát, amivel évtizedek óta nem látott ellenállást váltott ki.

stories.title: Nem a pénzhiány a baj, hanem az intézményi bénultság: ezért marad le a magyar tudomány
stories.summary: Egy európai kutatási projekt elindítása Belgiumban körülbelül egy hónap volt. Ugyanez Magyarországon másfél évig tartott. Nem a pénz hiányzott, és nem is az emberek. A különbséget az intézményi működés adta. Ez a magyar tudomány egyik legnagyobb, mégis ritkán kimondott problémája.

stories.title: Jobban keresnek a gen Z dolgozók, mint a millenniálok az ő korukban egy brit agytröszt szerint
stories.summary: Az új kutatás megállapította, hogy jobb pénzügyi feltételek mellett kezdik a karrierjüket a Z generációsok, mint az Y generációsok. De nem biztos, hogy ez így is marad.

stories.title: A Lidl új programja helyi kezdeményezéseket támogat a vásárlók bevonásával
stories.summary: A Lidl éves adományozási programot indít Magyarországon: ezúttal nem termékekkel vagy stratégiai partnerségekkel, hanem közvetlen pénzügyi támogatással segítené a civil szervezetek munkáját. Az Érték.Lánc Program olyan helyi kezdeményezéseket keres, amik valós problémákra reagálnak, mérhető eredményeket hoznak, és hosszabb távon változást hoznak a közösségek életében.

stories.title: Magyar Péter bejelentette a Tisztítótűz műveletet – élő közvetítés és elemzés
stories.summary: Egész napos élő adás az amerikai–iráni békekötés folyamatáról, Magyar Péter első EU-csúcsának tanulságairól és a rendkívüli parlamentről – a Telex újságíróinak elemzésével.

stories.summary: „Az iszapban járás kimerítő, és nem is túl gusztusos” – írta a tó átsétálásáról Kürti Gábor.

stories.title: Moving on from the sins of the Orbán regime too quickly would serve the interests of the perpetrators – Péter Krekó
stories.summary: More and more former servants of the Orbán system have been speaking out, but we haven’t heard nearly enough about the victims’ perspective, according to Péter Krekó. An interview with the political and behavioural scientist about the importance of Hungarian society coming to terms with the trauma of the past sixteen years.

stories.title: Kifizetné a „szivárványos rongyok” eltávolítása miatti bírságot Novák Előd
stories.summary: A Mi Hazánk mellett a Hatvannégy Vármegye Ifjúsági Mozgalom is felajánlotta segítségét a Pride-zászlókat a Dunába dobáló ismeretlennek.

stories.title: I challenge the Rothko naysayers to stand in front of his monumental art and not feel awe – Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
stories.summary: An exhibition in Florence that pairs his giant canvases with Renaissance religious art brought me to the edge of tears. It is the perfect refuge from the infinite scroll As an unbaptised agnostic raised with no religion, the closest I ever really come to a spiritual experience is when I’m standing in front of an artwork. Last week I went to Florence to do exactly that, drawn there not by Michelangelo’s David or Botticelli’s Birth of Venus, but by the works of Mark Rothko, that titan of US abstra

stories.title: Egyre jobban elmérgesedik a lengyel–ukrán vita, ami már nem várt helyeken is lecsapódik
stories.summary: Zelenszkij egy ukrán katonai egység címével régi történelmi sebeket tépett fel Lengyelországban, mire a lengyel elnök visszavonta a kitüntetését. Az orosz–ukrán háborúról ugyanazt gondolja a két ország, de a helyzet rendezetlenül tartós feszültségekhez vezethet.

stories.title: Az iráni kapus mindent fogott, második csoportmeccsén sem tudott győzni Belgium
stories.summary: A gól nélküli döntetlennek elsősorban az egyiptomiak és az új-zélandiak örülhetnek.

stories.title: Annyi embert rúgnak ki a nagy techcégek a mesterséges intelligencia miatt, hogy Kaliforniában a kormányzónak kellett közbelépni
stories.summary: Idén eddig 153 ezer embert építettek le az óriáscégek, mert feleslegessé váltak az MI miatt, vagy rajtuk spórolnak, hogy több pénz jusson az MI-re.

stories.title: Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
stories.summary: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the country's highest honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia began its war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
stories.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

stories.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee
stories.summary: The Czech government has approved a bill to put the funding of public service media under direct political control. Critics warn that if passed, the bill would put a pillar of Czech democracy at risk.

stories.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
stories.summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

stories.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the wo

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: Ukraine intensifies attacks on Crimea to raise cost of Russian occupation
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says strikes on oil facilities part of ‘long-range sanctions’ intended to isolate the territory Ukraine has stepped up its strikes on Crimea as part of a strategy to isolate the occupied peninsula from mainland Russia and raise the cost of the occupation. On Sunday, Russian-installed authorities suspended civilian fuel sales until at least Wednesday, a move that underscored Ukraine’s growing ability to disrupt supply lines linking Crimea to Russia. Continue reading...

stories.title: Andy Burnham on course to be British PM in weeks after Keir Starmer quits
stories.summary: LONDON — Andy Burnham, the man widely expected to replace Keir Starmer as U.K. prime minister, is on course to be running the country within weeks after a key rival for the Labour leadership folded and endorsed him. Starmer announced his resignation as British prime minister Monday morning, bowing to pressure after Burnham, the popular former mayor of Greater Manchester, resoundingly won a by-election that would let him challenge for the leadership of the governing Labour Party. In an at-times t

stories.title: Von der Leyen pays tribute to Starmer following his resignation announcement
stories.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen was among the first EU leaders to react to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s resignation announcement on Monday. In a warm message thanking “dear Keir,” the head of the EU’s executive praised the British leader for the accomplishments achieved during his less than two years in office. “It can take many leaders years to grow into the statesman you became in just two years,” wrote von der Leyen on X. The Commission president

stories.title: The US re-legalized the death penalty 50 years ago. Is it working as intended?
stories.summary: The Marshall Project analyzed over 9,000 death sentences handed down since states brought the punishment back Fifty years ago, Americans set out on a polarizing mission: to find a just and fair way to punish the worst-of-the-worst crimes by execution. In some ways, this was a surprising choice. In 1972, a narrow majority of the US supreme court had scrapped the country’s entire death penalty system, calling it “morally unacceptable”, “racially discriminatory” and “arbitrary”. It seemed possible

stories.title: Starmer has a strong green record – but a rightwing backlash weakened his plans
stories.summary: Prime minister was forced to row back on some policies despite strong support among voters for climate action Keir Starmer has faced a problem no Labour government has needed to deal with before. His energy and climate policies – core to solving the cost of living crisis – have come under attack from opposition parties, which have made dismantling the agenda one of their top priorities, second only to immigration, in their pitch to voters. This is new in British politics, where a cross-party con

stories.title: Zelenskyy pledges to ‘bring war back to Russia’ after drones swarm toward Moscow – Europe live
stories.summary: Russia intercepted 300 Ukrainian drones across the country and temporarily suspended operations from Moscow airport Brussels correspondent Meanwhile, the EU and the UK are “reassessing” a summit planned for 22 July, a European Commission spokesperson has said. “ It can take many leaders years to grow into the statesman you became in just two years. European and Ukrainian security is stronger because of you. Continue reading...

stories.title: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
stories.summary: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majority The Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” Abelardo de la Espriella has won Colombia’s presidential runoff, defeating the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda. With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, De la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A fur

stories.title: US-Iran talks strained as Trump threats spark Iranian walkout
stories.summary: Talks expected to continue for rest of the week despite disruption caused by US president’s threat to bomb Iran and kidnap negotiating team High-stakes talks between the US and Iran are expected to continue for the rest of the week in Switzerland, after a tense start that saw Iranian negotiators walk out in protest at a stream of threats issued by Donald Trump on social media. The US president had threatened to bomb Iran and even to kidnap the Iranian negotiating team unless the strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Teen accused of planning terror attack on Peter Dutton and Brisbane march was making ‘edgy joke’, court told
stories.summary: Defence barrister claims teenager was a ‘troubled kid’ but crown prosecutor says he was serious when he wrote of copying US domestic terrorists Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A teenager accused of preparing a nail bombing campaign was either a “troubled kid” who made edgy jokes or a determined terrorist in waiting, a court has been told. The teenager, who cannot be named due to his age, spent last week on trial in the Brisbane supreme court after pleading not guilty

stories.title: ‘Absolute nightmare’: Brexit bellwether constituencies revisited 10 years on
stories.summary: From north-east Scotland to Romford, London, what do those who spoke to the Guardian during the referendum campaign make of how it all panned out? The Guardian has revisited five bellwether constituencies we reported on during the 2016 EU referendum campaign, and asked those we spoke to at the time how they now feel about Brexit a decade on from the vote. stories.summary: The prime minister said a new leader will be in place before parliament returns in September Full report: Keir Starmer to step down as prime minister two years after historic election victory This is from Tom Baldwin , Keir Starmer’s biographer, and head of communications for Ed Miliband when he was Labour leader. We seem to be in a strange place where Keir Starmer is being told he must quit to prevent more uncertainty and chaos (by those who have caused much of it) but then stay on for a couple

stories.summary: The vice president said the move represented a ‘major milestone’ in ending Iran’s nuclear programme According to Palestinian news agency Wafa , a high school student was killed and several other civilians were injured earlier today in an Israeli attack on a civilian vehicle in Gaza City. The Gaza health ministry says at least 1,021 people have been killed in Israeli attacks since the ‘ceasefire’ between Israel and Hamas came into effect in October 2025. In a post on X, Pakistan’s prime minister,

stories.title: At least seven people killed in Chicago shootings as Trump renews military call
stories.summary: Mayor says ‘violence has no place in our city’ as president criticizes governor for not accepting national guard troops At least seven people have been killed and dozens injured in several shootings in Chicago since Friday, police said, with Donald Trump once again calling for military intervention in the midwestern city. In a post on Truth Social, Trump questioned why Illinois’s governor, JB Pritzker, had not welcomed military deployment. Continue reading...

stories.title: US-backed political newcomer wins Colombia election, marking country's right-wing shift
stories.summary: With nearly all votes counted, the election results polarised the country, sparking both celebrations and protests across the country.

stories.title: Former Kenyan justice minister blocked from entering Uganda, lawyers' body says
stories.summary: Martha Karua is a lawyer representing detained Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye who is on trial for treason.

stories.title: To build climate resilience, start with food
stories.summary: As London Climate Action Week brings together leaders from across Europe and beyond, the urgency of climate action is unmistakable. What matters now is not adding new targets but making choices that have a measurable impact on reducing emissions and building food system resilience. Food systems are where climate impacts are felt most immediately. When food prices rise, when supply chains falter, when waste increases, the effects reach households, public services and political trust at once. That

stories.title: Big gas is trying to cash in on Europe’s fossil fuel crisis
stories.summary: As Europe grapples with spiking energy prices and debates how to strengthen its energy system, one issue has become the wrong target of criticism: methane rules. The EU Methane Regulation decreases gas waste by requiring fossil fuel companies to monitor, report and reduce methane emissions — the second most potent greenhouse gas after CO2. It is the world’s first binding legislation of its kind, applying not only to domestic producers but also to imported fossil fuels. If fully implemented, it c

stories.title: Iran-US peace talks show ‘encouraging progress,’ say mediators
stories.summary: Qatari and Pakistani mediators said on Monday that negotiations on a U.S.-Iran peace deal showed “encouraging progress” and that talks will continue at a technical level. The statement came after the U.S., Iran and the two mediating countries concluded talks in Switzerland, which marked the first high-level meeting between Washington and Tehran since the start of the war in late February. The two countries signed a preliminary agreement last week aimed at ending hostilities, opening

stories.title: Revealed: Brexit voting areas have seen faster growth in foreign workers since EU referendum
stories.summary: Guardian investigation also finds same areas experienced relative decline over same period Leave-voting areas have seen faster relative growth in foreign workers since the Brexit referendum, a Guardian investigation has found. Data analysis suggests that the decade since the Brexit vote may not have matched the expectations of many Leave supporters, showing their local areas have also become relatively more deprived over the same period. Continue reading...

stories.title: LA firefighters battle warehouse blaze amid concerns over billowing smoke
stories.summary: Newsom declares state of emergency as crews continue to fight stubborn Boyle Heights fire that has raged for days California’s governor has declared a state of emergency for the city of Los Angeles, as firefighters struggle to contain a stubborn warehouse blaze that has raged for days and blanketed parts of the city in smoke. Gavin Newsom announced he was directing state agencies to provide “additional assistance and resources” to help battle the fire, located in the neighborhood of Boyle Height

stories.title: The aftershocks of Brexit’s failure could be gaining strength – a fearful prospect for Ireland | Fintan O’Toole
stories.summary: On our side of the Irish Sea we have made the best of a bad job. But we saw the damage a reckless and reactionary British government could do To read more from the Brexit Vote: 10 Years On series, click here For Brexit’s true believers, Ireland will always be the spoke in the wheel that set everything off course, the green tarnish that took the shine off the golden age. stories.title: Brexit: how it has hit your wallet at the supermarket and on holiday
stories.summary: Ten years on, leaving the EU has made life more difficult and costly – here are some of the ways we’ve lost out It is 10 years since voters in the UK chose to leave the EU, and our wallets have been feeling the effects ever since. From paying more to take the dog on holidays in France – and making calls while you are there – to higher grocery bills and the headache of filling in customs forms for parcels, Brexit has made many simple tasks more complicated and expensive. stories.title: Paris taxi scam cost £493 but Monzo won’t help me
stories.summary: We were charged the wrong amount, but because the bank says we have no evidence it won’t do a chargeback I went to Paris to recover from the grief of losing my dog. All was going well until I took a taxi from a rank outside Musée d’Orsay to my hotel near Notre Dame – a 12-minute journey. Continue reading...

stories.title: Marius Borg Høiby rape conviction renews focus in Norway on consent in digital age
stories.summary: Norway is supposedly one of world’s most gender-equal countries, yet sexual violence remains prevalent across society In many ways, the case of Marius Borg Høiby, who was sentenced to four years in prison last week after being found guilty of offences including domestic violence and two counts of rape, was exceptional. stories.title: I tried the new soft drinks from Trump’s son and granddaughter. Bad!
stories.summary: Barron Trump has a new yerba mate and Kai Trump a ‘bold’ energy drink – would their offerings pass the taste test? Do you like sugary soft drinks? stories.summary: Investment company Castlelake made bid public for shareholders to evaluate but carrier describes offer as ‘cheap’ Business live – latest updates The US investment firm trying to buy easyJet has gone public with its latest £4.7bn takeover proposal for the budget airline, its third and latest offer to be rejected. stories.summary: Labour leader bows to mounting pressure after Andy Burnham’s success against Reform UK in Makerfield UK politics live – latest updates Keir Starmer has announced he will stand down as prime minister after days of intense pressure from Labour MPs, paving the way for Andy Burnham to take over at Downing Street. stories.title: UK Met Office issues rare red weather warning for Wednesday and Thursday
stories.summary: People in southern Wales and area of England from Kent and Sussex to Somerset and Birmingham urged to protect themselves from extreme heat The UK’s Met Office forecasters have issued a rare red weather warning for Wednesday and Thursday in the face of extreme heat and humidity. Temperatures are set to reach 44C in some areas and authorities warning of risks to health, travel and public services. How are you coping with the extreme heat? stories.title: Colombians vote in runoff election expected to trigger shift in decades-long armed conflict
stories.summary: Frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups Ghost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff vote Colombians are going to the polls in a presidential runoff expected to trigger a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict, now at its most violent point since the landmark 2016 peace agreement between the government and most of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Polls sh

stories.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
stories.summary: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. stories.title: Deniz Undav: Germany's 2026 World Cup supersub
stories.summary: With three goals in two games, striker Deniz Undav is the man in focus for Germany at the World Cup. But things could have turned out very differently for the Stuttgart striker.

stories.title: Colombia election: Hard-right candidate claims victory
stories.summary: Right-wing presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, won 49.66% of the Colombians' votes, according to preliminary results.

stories.title: US-Iran talks end in Switzerland, mediators say
stories.summary: According to mediators from Qatar and Pakistan, the talks between United States and Iranian officials in Switzerland have ended with both sides agreeing on a roadmap. Technical talks will continue.

stories.title: Germany news: Could heatwave temperatures hit 40°C?
stories.summary: Germany is in the grip of an intense heatwave, with experts predicting extreme temperatures in some regions. Chancellor Friedrich Merz insists he can handle the heat and is not bothered by personal insults. DW has more.

stories.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
stories.summary: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

stories.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
stories.summary: In an emotional speech at 10 Downing Street, Keir Starmer said he will step down after days of mounting pressure and speculation over his future. It comes as leadership contender Andy Burnham returns to parliament.

stories.title: India news: NEET retest saw millions of students turn up
stories.summary: More than 2 million students appeared for the high-stake NEET medical entrance exam after the question paper leak fiasco amid high security. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: US-Iran talks: Mediators hail 'roadmap' for final agreement
stories.summary: Members of the international community have welcomed progress in talks between the US and Iran to end the war on all fronts. In an early sign of optimism, oil prices fell in later trading after the news. DW has more.

stories.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big
stories.summary: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

stories.title: Former US Fed chair Alan Greenspan dies aged 100
stories.summary: Alan Greenspan oversaw the US Federal Reserve for nearly two decades from 1987 until 2006. Critics argue that his policies contributed to the Global Financial Crisis of 2007.

stories.title: UK's Starmer mulls 'political realities' after Burnham by-election
stories.summary: A member of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet said it would be "delusional" to pretend there was no threat to his position. But he also said he had no information suggesting he might quit, as a newspaper reported.

stories.title: Scottish police arrest man over Edinburgh attacks
stories.summary: Five people were injured in the attacks on Friday that were described by Prime Minister Keir Starmer as being motivated by "anti-Muslim hatred."

stories.title: Ethiopia's PM Abiy Ahmed: From Nobel winner to war commander
stories.summary: Abiy Ahmed will remain Ethiopia's prime minister after his party won the elections yet again. Since taking office in 2018, he has attracted both strong supporters and fierce critics, reflecting his polarizing leadership.

stories.title: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war
stories.summary: As ticket demand outstrips supply by a ratio of 15 to one, desperate fans have become easy targets.

stories.title: Cape Verde fan goes wild live on BBC News as his country scores
stories.summary: During a live BBC News broadcast, reporter Paul Njie interviews a Cape Verde fan when the country suddenly scores its first ever World Cup goal against Uruguay in the 2026 World Cup.

stories.title: Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas
stories.summary: If Kostyantynivka falls, Russian forces would be able push towards Ukraine's last remaining strongholds in the east.

stories.title: Trump says repairs to algae-plagued Reflecting Pool will begin immediately
stories.summary: Despite a multi-million dollar renovation, the Washington DC landmark has peeling paint and algae. Trump claims vandals are partly to blame.

stories.title: Largest ever cocaine bust in Australia after police raid underground bunker
stories.summary: Police seized 2.7 tonnes of cocaine worth an estimated A$816m after searching a property in western Sydney.

stories.title: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

stories.title: Lower-level technical talks to continue as first round of direct US-Iran negotiations wrap up
stories.summary: Iran’s top diplomat hailed mediators Pakistan and Qatar’s efforts over what he called “major progress” to end the continued Israeli offensive on Lebanon, despite a fragile truce. The comments came after Iran and the US kickstarted a 6-day negotiating window to permanently end the war.

stories.title: Japan quintuples visa fees in first price hike since 1978
stories.summary: Authorities say they do not expect the hikes to have an "immediate impact on inbound tourism".

stories.title: Major explosion at Qatar's largest energy site injures dozens as 18 remain unaccounted for
stories.summary: Qatar’s Interior Ministry says search and rescue operations are continuing to locate close to two dozen people who remain missing following the large explosion at the country’s main energy site.

stories.title: China's import of custard apples is sparking fears in Taiwan
stories.summary: Taiwan's agriculture ministry is worried that Beijing wants to weaponise a local specialty: the atemoya.

stories.title: Spain pound Saudi Arabia 4-0 as Egypt tops Group G in matchday two fixtures of the 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Spain returns to its winning ways as Egypt break their group’s draw deadlock in the second matchday fixtures for groups G and H at the 2026 World Cup.

stories.title: Australian police seize nearly 3 tonnes of cocaine in what has become the largest bust on record
stories.summary: The bust of nearly 3 tonnes is the country’s largest ever and is believed to hold a street value of just shy of €500 million.

stories.title: Norway fans take over Times Square with Viking row
stories.summary: Norwegian supporters in Viking helmets and national colours packed Times Square, chanting and singing before Norway’s Group I match against Senegal.

stories.title: China announces sanctions on 10 US companies as trade tensions flare
stories.summary: The sanctions come a month after US President Donald Trump visited Beijing for talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping aimed at stabilising relations.

stories.title: Three dead in Philippines high school shooting over bullying 'grudge'
stories.summary: Police say they believe the attack was motivated by a "grudge" over bullying.

stories.title: Late singer Oliver Tree's body returned to US after helicopter crash
stories.summary: The 32-year-old California native died whilst on his world tour, ahead of a date in Brazil.

stories.title: Schools close as more than half of France under red heat alerts
stories.summary: The health minister warns many citizens "will suffer", with temperatures set to peak on Monday.

stories.title: First round of US-Iran talks ends with encouraging progress, mediators say
stories.summary: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.

stories.title: US and Iran publicly trade threats as talks take place in Switzerland
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump on Sunday threatened to strike Iran if it did not stop Hezbollah from "causing trouble", while Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned the US to "be careful with their statements".

stories.title: Currency crash and visa crackdowns force Indian students to rethink studying abroad
stories.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

stories.title: Starmer quits, Streeting backs Burnham — live updates
stories.summary: Andy Burnham, the man widely expected to replace Keir Starmer as U.K. prime minister, has won the endorsement of a key Labour leadership rival — paving the way for him to lead the country within weeks. Starmer announced his departure as British prime minister Monday, bowing to pressure after Labour colleague Burnham resoundingly won a by-election that would let him challenge for the top job. In a tearful statement outside No.10 Downing Street, Starmer said he had informed King Charles III o

stories.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
stories.summary: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country. For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubble, logs and debris in protest against the conserv

stories.title: ‘There’s no jobs’: struggle and regret in a Welsh town that backed Brexit
stories.summary: Ten years ago Ebbw Vale had the highest proportion of leave voters in Wales despite huge EU funding, which has not been fully replaced Where Ebbw Vale’s steelworks once stood is now a cluster of gleaming modern buildings including a hospital, a leisure centre and a college. Over the past decade, these public facilities have been joined by a public-private cybersecurity research centre and two tech firms. A new railway station opened at the site in 2015. Yet, during the Guardian’s visit to the We

stories.title: Russian strikes on eastern Ukraine kill three and injure 22
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Saturday that his forces had targeted an oil-processing facility in Russia's Tyumen region.

stories.title: Trump faces fresh bipartisan criticism on Iran deal as Vance hails peace talks
stories.summary: Objections come as Trump threatens to renew attacks on Iran if it doesn’t rein in its proxy in Lebanon US political figures from left and right voiced fresh objections on Sunday to Donald Trump’s provisional deal with Iran – even as the US president made new threats while Vice-President JD Vance hailed progress during the first round of direct peace talks in Switzerland. Negotiations in Lucerne between the US and Iran have already run into difficulties, after Trump wrote on Truth Social that “Ir

stories.title: Planes were just 300ft apart in Boston airport near miss, expert says
stories.summary: FAA investigating incident involving Delta and American jets that forced Delta plane to abort landing attempt A Delta jet was roughly 300ft (90 meters) from an American Airlines plane during a close call at Boston’s airport that forced the Delta aircraft to abort a weekend landing attempt, an aviation expert said on Sunday. The Federal Aviation Administration said it was investigating the incident between two commercial flights that happened Saturday at Boston Logan international airport. Contin

stories.title: Germany's media rocked by AI scandal
stories.summary: Two German outlets deleted articles that used undisclosed artificial intelligence. Many fear that reliance on AI will damage the credibility of German media

stories.title: Ethiopia's ruling party wins overwhelming majority in parliament
stories.summary: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed’s Prosperity Party comfortably secured another parliamentary majority, defeating a fragmented opposition.

stories.title: Severe heat wave sweeps Europe, triggering alerts and disruptions
stories.summary: Authorities accross Europe have warned of temperatures exceeding 39 and 40 degrees Celsius as a 'heat dome' sets in. Germany also faces the threat of extreme thunderstorms.

stories.title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
stories.summary: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.

stories.title: Parisians cool off in canal amid 'red alert' heatwave
stories.summary: France has issued red heatwave alerts for around half the country as temperatures soar to record levels.

stories.title: Is Germany looking again at coal-powered electricity?
stories.summary: It had planned to abandon the fuel, but the higher cost of natural gas may make it think again.

stories.title: Új-Zéland–Egyiptom 1-3
Nem sokkal a letartóztatások előtt szerződött 9 milliárdra a XIII. kerület a parkfenntartási botrány egyik cégével
stories.summary: A szerződést utólag csak akkor lehetne felmondani, ha a cég nem vagy rosszul végezné a munkáját. A kerület több tízmilliárd forintra szerződött a vállalattal az elmúlt években. Ez már a negyedik sokmilliárdos szerződés a felek között.

stories.title: 2027-ben indul a világ leghosszabb direkt repülőjárata London és Sydney között, 22 óra lesz az út
stories.summary: Speciálisan átalakított Airbus A350-esek fognak járni az útvonalon, ezekbe 20 ezer literrel több kerozint lehet tankolni a szokásosnál.

stories.title: A Zöld-foki-szigetek válogatottja nem bír leállni, Uruguayt is sokkolták, és simán továbbjuthatnak
stories.summary: A Zöld-foki-szigetek hatalmas szabadrúgásgóllal szerzett vezetést, aztán Uruguay hiába fordított, nem bírt a vb eddigi meglepetéscsapatával.

stories.title: Egészen más az étel íze, ha forrón vagy hidegen esszük
stories.summary: Nyáron hidegen is megesszük a tökfőzeléket, sőt egy csomó mást is – de hogyan befolyásolja az ízérzékelésünket az, hogy egy étel fagyos, forró, langyos, esetleg ezek keveréke?

stories.title: Új-Zéland ráijesztett Egyiptomra, de Szaláhék a második félidőben összehozták a történelmi sikert
stories.summary: Egyiptom két kör után vezeti a csoportját, miután remek második félidei teljesítménnyel megfordították az Új-Zéland elleni meccset.

stories.title: Késések a győri vonalon felsővezeték-szakadás, a dél-balatoni vonalon váltóhiba miatt
stories.summary: Biatorbágy és Herceghalom között pedig csak az egyik vágányon lehet közlekedni.

stories.title: Felhőszakadás és jégeső is jöhet a kánikula mellé hétfőn
stories.summary: A hét második felére már szárazság várható, hétvégére a 40 fokot is megközelítheti a hőmérséklet.

stories.title: A zöld-foki bravúrkapus, Vozinha anyja megkapta a vízumot, így már a helyszínen ünnepelte a vb meglepetéscsapatát
stories.summary: Vozinha korábban arra panaszkodott, hogy túl drága volt a vízum, de a FIFA és a Zöld-foki Köztársaság végül rendezte a dolgot, így Ana Candida Evora már Miamiban nézte fia meccsét.

stories.title: „A Dunába is dobhatnám” – egy vallomás szerint Pék, a politikus több százmilliót vett át Karácsony nevében, aztán nem intézett el semmit
stories.summary: Több mint 600 millió forintot fizetett ki a politikusnak Z. Zsolt parkfenntartási vállalkozó az óbudai korrupciós ügyben született, ellentmondásosnak tűnő vallomása szerint. A rendkívül költekező életmódú politikus azt ígérte, a főváros vezetésénél is elintéz dolgokat, a főpolgármester azonban nemhogy nem adott megbízásokat, de az összes többi parkfenntartási céget is kidobta a fővárosi közterületekről.

stories.title: 2,7 tonna kokain egy földalatti bunkerben: Ausztrália legnagyobb kokainfogására bukkant a rendőrség
stories.summary: A helyszínen két, húszas éveiben járó férfit tartóztattak le, ha a bíróság elítéli őket, életfogytiglani börtönbüntetést kaphatnak.

stories.title: Biztatóan zárult az iráni–amerikai tárgyalások első napja Svájcban
stories.summary: Vasárnap ültek össze a felek Katar és Pakisztán közvetítésével, akik előrehaladásról számoltak be.

stories.title: Nagyot zuhant Matolcsy Ádám bútoros cége, 836 milliós veszteséggel zárta a tavalyi évet
stories.summary: A Balaton Bútor exportbevételei viszont növekedtek, a legnagyobbat az Egyesült Arab Emírségekben ugrott a kereslet.

stories.title: Több ukrán városban is dróntámadásokban haltak meg civilek, az oroszok több tucat drónt lőttek le Moszkva közelében
stories.summary: Az ukrajnai Szumi régiójában egy család három tagja is életét vesztette. Közben a Krím félszigeten korlátozták az áramhasználatot.

stories.title: A zöld-fokiak edzője felhúzta magát az uruguayi játékoson, aki otthagyta az ellenfél sérültjét a földön
stories.summary: Federico Viñas simán otthagyta a földön fekvő Telmo Arcanjót, a támadásból pedig gólt szerzett Uruguay.

stories.title: Több tucatnyian megsérültek, miután robbanás történt Katar fő LNG-feldolgozó üzemében
stories.summary: Tizennyolc embert eltűntként tartanak számon a hatóságok, a QatarEnergy egyelőre nem közölte, hogy az üzemben keletkeztek-e károk.

stories.title: Serena Williams belefáradt az otthoni ücsörgésbe, visszatért, és játszik a wimbledoni főtáblán
stories.summary: 44 évesen kapott szabad kártyát, az első forduló slágermeccse lesz.

stories.title: Lemondott Keir Starmer brit miniszterelnök
stories.summary: Amíg pártja meg nem találja utódját, marad kormányfő, és mindent meg fog tenni, hogy a hatalomátadás a lehető legrendezettebben történjen meg.

stories.title: A hír igaz – jelentette be az ecsegi polgármester, hogy Gyurcsány Ferenc a faluba költözik
stories.summary: A volt miniszterelnök is megszólalt az ügyben.

stories.title: Conceição szerint nincs nyomás a portugálokon, hogy mindenképp Ronaldóhoz passzoljanak
stories.summary: Továbbra is a nagy Ronaldo-dilemma a fő téma a portugáloknál. A játékosok barátnőit is összeugrasztó vita után Conceição védte meg Ronaldót.

stories.title: Több mint fesztivál: így telik egy nap a Paloznaki Jazzpikniken (x)
stories.summary: Rick Astley-koncert, modern jazz a naplementében, divatbemutató a szőlőtőkék közelében, balatoni borok és családi programok egyetlen fesztiválon. A Paloznaki Jazzpiknik évről évre több, mint 20 ezer látogatót vonz, de aki először érkezik, gyakran nem is tudja, milyen varázslatos világba csöppen. Megmutatjuk, hogyan néz ki egy nap Paloznakon.

stories.title: Már több brit politikus is reagált Starmer lemondására, Nigel Farage előrehozott választást követel
stories.summary: A Konzervatív Párt vezetője szerint Starmer borzalmas miniszterelnök volt, de nem csak Starmer jelent problémát.

stories.title: Sulyok Tamás nem árulja el, milyen megkereséssel és érvekkel fordult a Velencei Bizottsághoz
stories.summary: A Sándor-palota megtagadta a választ a TASZ közérdekű adatigénylésére.

stories.title: Mit szóltak a magzatelhajtáshoz Horthyék?
stories.summary: Gyerekpárti propaganda és nemzethalál. Egy biciklire pattanó időutazó és egy fotós, akit közel engedtek magukhoz a nomád pásztorok. Heti könyvajánló.

stories.title: 40 fokos hőhullámra készül Európa, Franciaországban már hárman meghaltak
stories.summary: Több napos hőségriadót jelentettek be Angliában is, ahol 38 fok is lehet.

stories.title: A Tisztítótűz művelet részeként kezdeményezik Sulyok Tamás elnöki megbízatásának megszűntetését is
stories.summary: Tisztítótűz müvelet néven átfogó akció indul „a politikai és gazdasági maffia” felszámolására. Lemondatják az államfőt, szeptemberben átfogó alkotmányozási folyamat indul. A parlamenti képviselői mandátumot is korlátoznák, 12 évig, három cikluson át lehetne bárki képviselő.

stories.title: Kemény, másként pattan a labda, lelassul a játék, túl rövid a fű – kritizálják a vb-döntő gyepszőnyegét
stories.summary: Edzők és játékosok panaszkodtak az East Rutherford-i stadion pályájára, de a FIFA szerint minden rendben van.

stories.title: Baleset miatt csak egy vágányon közlekedik a ráckevei HÉV
stories.summary: A katasztrófavédelem szerint a HÉV egy kisteherautóval ütközött.

stories.title: Veszprém mutathatja meg, milyen jövő vár a NER-es építőiparra
stories.summary: A választások után nyert 24 milliárdos közbeszerzést a NER egyik fontos építőcége. A pénz azonban még nincs meg a projekthez, azt a kormánytól várnák. Érdekes tesztje lehet ez a szektornak.

stories.title: Two-thirds of EU citizens back UK rejoining bloc, survey finds
stories.summary: Poll also finds three quarters of people in Britain want closer ties, with majority accepting free movement Two-thirds of EU citizens would back Britain rejoining the bloc, while most UK voters say Brexit has been bad for the issues they care about and want closer ties, including levels of integration – such as free movement – long seen as toxic, a survey has found. Ten years after the Brexit referendum, the polling by the European Council on Foreign Relations (ECFR) , a thinktank, found 66% of

stories.title: Canada’s policies force asylum seekers into US to face deportation, critics say
stories.summary: Advocates say the Safe Third Country Agreement forces immigrants to head to an unsafe country: the United States It was the threat of gang violence in Honduras that pushed Carlos and Antonia to flee their home. In 2021, with their toddler, Alejandro, and a handful of belongings, the married couple ventured north hoping to reach safety in the US. The journey, through Guatemala and Mexico , was filled with danger and uncertainty . Continue reading...

stories.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
stories.summary: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

stories.title: Spain: PM's wife must face corruption trial, judge rules
stories.summary: A court in Madrid ruled that there is sufficient evidence against Begona Gomez, the wife of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez. The 55-year-old university director is banned from leaving the country.

stories.title: Zelenskyy issues one-week ultimatum to Lukashenka over drone-guidance equipment
stories.summary: During a media briefing in Kyiv on Friday, Zelenskyy threatened Ukraine will take down the drone relay equipment on the border if the Belarusian leader, Aliaksandr Lukashenka, doesn't do anything to address it.

stories.title: Trump threatens Hormuz tolls as ships sail on despite IRGC's closure claim
stories.summary: Iran's IRGC said it had closed the Strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes on Lebanon, but CENTCOM said 55 ships carrying 17 million barrels of oil transited unhindered. Trump meanwhile warned of US tolls if a final deal was not reached within 60 days.

stories.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
stories.summary: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
stories.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

stories.title: Colombia's escalating, brutal internal conflict is defining its presidential election
stories.summary: A left-wing senator who backs talks with armed gangs faces an outsider endorsed by Trump.

stories.title: US-Iran peace deal talks underway in Switzerland
stories.summary: "We're going to hopefully make progress on the nuclear issue, make progress on the Lebanon ceasefire issue," US Vice President JD Vance told reporters.

stories.title: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.title: Ghost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff vote
stories.summary: Colombians will choose on Sunday between two men whose lives have been very differently shaped by the militias, and whose visions for the country are poles apart Whoever wins Sunday’s presidential runoff vote in Colombia, the country’s next leader will have a personal history intertwined with one of the criminal forces at the heart of a decades-long armed conflict that claimed nearly half a million lives. The lives of Iván Cepeda and Abelardo de la Espriella have, in very different ways, been sh

stories.title: Belgium–Irán 0-0
Kétségbeejtő állapotokat mutat be a János Kórház pszichiátriai osztályán a 24.hu riportja
stories.summary: Hegedűs Zsolt egészségügyi miniszter már a megjelenése előtt reagált a videóra.

stories.title: A szerelmes lányok szomorúbbak, mint gondolnád
stories.summary: Olivia Rodrigo eddigi legérettebb és koherensebb lemeze, a You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love bizonyítéka annak, hogy nem szégyen szeretni, majd kudarcot vallani egy kapcsolatban.

stories.title: Vissza se juttok a kib... országotokba! – üzente Trump a Hormuzi-szorosról tárgyaló irániaknak
stories.summary: Amerikai elnök ezzel akart lendületet adni a Vance alelnök vezette amerikai delegációnak az iráni féllel való svájci tárgyaláshoz. Az irániak szerint Trump fenyegetése a kétségbeesés jele.

stories.title: Meghalt a Doom és a Duke Nukem legendás zeneszerzője
stories.summary: Bobby Prince 81 éves volt. Az 1990-es években olyan hangokat csiholt ki a korabeli pécékből, mint senki más.

stories.title: Óriási versenyben találta meg Izland a világ legrosszabb fotósát
stories.summary: Nem könnyű 127 ezerből a legutolsónak lenni, egy 28 éves francia nőnek sikerült az izlandi légitársaság versenyén.

stories.title: Uruguay–Zöld-foki Köztársaság 2-2
France bans alcohol during Fete de la Musique amid heat wave
stories.summary: France is taking additional measures as millions of people gear up for Fete de la Musique under punishing temperatures.

stories.title: Are Ukraine drones really exposing gaps in Russia's defense?
stories.summary: Once seen as almost impenetrable, Russian air defenses are now under growing strain from Ukrainian drones. A recent attack on Moscow has reignited debate over Russian vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Terror police probe suspected anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh
stories.summary: Counterterrorism police have taken charge after a series of violent attacks in the Scottish capital that left five men injured.

stories.title: Undav heroics help Germany beat Ivory Coast
stories.summary: In Germany's second 2026 World Cup game, Deniz Undav helped Germany to a comeback win against Ivory Coast in Toronto.

stories.title: Germany could scrap law banning insults against politicians
stories.summary: Germany's special law prohibiting the insulting of politicians has led to cases that many people consider absurd. Some members of the government want to abolish the law; others think there's a good reason to retain it.

stories.title: Stonehenge revelers ring in summer solstice
stories.summary: Thousands celebrated through the night at Stonehenge in southwest England, ushering in the sunrise on the longest day of the year in the Northern Hemisphere. The stone circle aligns with the sun's path at the solstices.

stories.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

stories.title: Tourist dies in Dominican Republic luxury resort fire
stories.summary: Nearly 1,700 people were evacuated from the huge blaze at Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach in Bayahibe, a popular resort town on the Caribbean coast.

stories.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
stories.summary: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died after several days in hospital, an environmental group said.

stories.title: Meet Osito, the rescue dog winning hearts at the World Cup
stories.summary: A rescue dog named Osito has become an unlikely World Cup star in Mexico City, attracting crowds, selfies and social media attention wherever he goes.

stories.title: Why an AI company cleaned my New York City apartment for free
stories.summary: An AI company is sending free cleaners door-to-door in a bid to train the robots it hopes one day will replace them.

stories.title: At CrimeCon true crime obsessives come face-to-face with real loss
stories.summary: Victims' families say there are ways to be an ethical true-crime fan - not an exploitative one.

stories.title: Backstage at Gorillaz' epic, one-off stadium show: 'The vibe is ridiculous'
stories.summary: Damon Albarn, De La Soul and Moonchild Sannelly talk backstage as Gorillaz play their biggest show.

stories.title: Former Olympian denies vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool after arrest
stories.summary: Davey Hearn says he was simply touching the new paint at the site out of curiosity and did not remove or alter it.

stories.title: Dutch royals enjoy two big results in one World Cup day
stories.summary: There may be about 5,000 miles between the Netherlands and their former colony Curacao but members of the Dutch royal family managed to see both sides play on Saturday.

stories.title: ‘A flying White House’: President Trump unveils the new Air Force One
stories.summary: Converted from a former Qatari-owned Boeing 747, the new Air Force One will serve as a temporary presidential aircraft until Boeing delivers the long-delayed replacement fleet in 2028.

stories.title: Tight security as Indian students resit medical exam after alleged paper leak
stories.summary: Millions of candidates face biometric checks and frisking as air force deployed to secure exam papers.

stories.title: Indian cricketer, 15, hits record-breaking 50 in just 11 balls
stories.summary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks the record for the fastest 50-over List A half-century in history on the same day he was not included in India's one-day international squad.

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Ukrainian strikes on a Moscow oil refinery, protests at the G7 summit, wildfires in Spain and Messi at the World Cup – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at landmark Ghana conference
stories.summary: Call for formal apologies from countries that benefited from transatlantic trade slave included in 19-point roadmap More than money: the logic of slavery reparations A global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade. Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah agreed to renew ceasefire, officials say
stories.summary: Lebanese media reported an Israeli airstrike in Southern Lebanon on Friday, after a US official said Israel and Hezbollah had agreed on a ceasefire.

stories.title: 16 dead and 12 injured following Israeli strikes, Lebanon's civil defence agency says
stories.summary: It comes less than a day after US officials said Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah had agreed to a renewed ceasefire.

stories.title: 'Neither I nor Italy ever beg': Giorgia Meloni rejects Trump's claim she begged for a photo – video
stories.summary: The Italian prime minister posted a video on X saying that Donald Trump's claim she begged him for a photo was 'completely made up'. The US president made the comment to Italy’s La7 TV channel after the G7 summit in France, saying: 'She wanted a picture with me so badly. I wouldn’t have taken it, but I felt sorry for her' Italy PM Meloni ‘stunned’ by Trump’s claims she begged him for a photo Continue reading...

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
stories.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

stories.title: Women tackling coal dust and other eco wins this week
stories.summary: Indian women fight back against toxic coal dust, the US hits a solar milestone, and there's good news for soil in Kenya and cars worldwide.

stories.title: Can Europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
stories.summary: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

stories.title: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life. But can they adapt quickly enough?
stories.summary: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life.

stories.title: What to expect from the strongest El Nino in over a century
stories.summary: Warnings of a mighty El Nino weather pattern this year could bring drought, flooding and extreme heat. Can we prepare for the worst of it?

stories.title: German journalist detained in Syria back home, lawyer says
stories.summary: Eva Maria Michelmann vanished in Syria in January as government forces advanced on Kurdish fighters in the northeast. The German government last week said it was working to get her home.

stories.title: EU ban on BPA chemical in food packaging takes effect
stories.summary: From July 2026 onwards, BPA will no longer be permitted in EU food packaging. The industrial chemical is known to interfere with the human hormonal system.

stories.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
stories.summary: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

stories.title: Tehran says Strait of Hormuz remains open after confusion
stories.summary: The Persian Gulf Strait Authority has announced that ships that submit "compliant transit requests" would be allowed to transit the Strait.

stories.title: New Russian strikes hit Kharkiv and Odesa, killing one and injuring children
stories.summary: New Russian strikes hit Ukraine's Kharkiv and Odesa regions on 19 June, killing one person, injuring children and adults, and damaging homes, vehicles and fuel facilities overnight.

stories.title: As Europe bakes, Berlin Zoo’s elephants and bears cool off
stories.summary: Berlin Zoo uses “ice bombs” and water showers to help bears and elephants cool down as Europe faces an intense heatwave this weekend.

stories.title: Heatwave nears 40C in France, forcing tourists to change routines
stories.summary: France is enduring a widespread heatwave, with temperatures nearing 40C in parts of the country and forcing tourists and residents to change their routines.

stories.title: Would you choose to take a 22-hour non-stop flight?
stories.summary: The BBC asked Sydney locals if they would take the newly announced, longest ever commercial flight from Sydney to London.

stories.title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
stories.summary: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

stories.title: Japanese pop group XG went from brutal five year training to global pop stars
stories.summary: The Japanese pop group have become global stars, after being recruited before they were teenagers.

stories.title: James Burrows, legendary director of Cheers and Friends, dies aged 85
stories.summary: Best-known as co-creator of sitcom Cheers, Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of other classics including the Big Bang Theory and Will and Grace.

stories.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
stories.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

stories.title: Australia confirms first case of H5N1 bird flu as virus reaches every continent
stories.summary: Australia was previously the only continent where the H5N1 bird flu strain had not yet been found.

stories.title: Italian tourist dies in Dominican Republic resort blaze
stories.summary: Around 1,690 tourists were evacuated from a luxury resort in the village of Bayahibe after a fire broke out.

stories.title: Steve Rosenberg: Moscow oil refinery attack brings Russia's war with Ukraine closer to home
stories.summary: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.

stories.title: Rightwing MEPs chant 'send them back' after controversial deportation plans are passed – video
stories.summary: Several lawmakers celebrated a vote in the European parliament approving plans to increase deportations of undocumented people. Chants of 'send them back' spurred other MEPs to chant back 'shame on you'. The plans could lead to ICE-style immigration measures being implemented in Europe, which have been criticised by rights groups Continue reading...
